title: AdapterProtocol
description:
The schema root aggregating every wire type. It exists to generate one
committed JSON schema (and the Python SDK models); its optional client
fields are never all populated in a single message.

AdapterError.description: A structured rejection an integration returns in an [`AdapterResponse::Error`].

AdapterErrorCode.description: Machine-readable failure category an adapter reports in an [`AdapterError`].

oneOf.description: The request was malformed or missing required fields.
The request's protocol version is not accepted.
A framework setting was unknown or invalid.
An unexpected internal failure occurred in the integration.
The requested operation is not supported by this integration.

AdapterRequest.description:
The one JSON request an integration reads from stdin, tagged by the
requested operation.

oneOf.description:
Plan a serve topology: declare roles, per-replica requirements, links,
and endpoint requirements from the requested shape.

Render final process invocations for a planned topology, given the
control plane's concrete process allocations.

AdapterResponse.description: The one JSON response an integration writes to stdout, tagged by outcome.

oneOf.description: The operation succeeded and carries its result.
The operation was rejected with a structured error.

AdapterResult.description:
The successful result of an [`AdapterRequest`], tagged by the operation it
answers.

oneOf.description: The planned topology from a `PlanServe` request.
The rendered process invocations from a `RenderServe` request.

AllocationLaunch.description: The machine-local launch channel selected by the control plane.

BenchCaseInput.description: A single Bench case: its load shape and the number of requests to send.

BenchClientRequest.description:
The request the Bench measurement runtime passes to its client: the
endpoint, model, bench definition, the load case to run, and the artifact
directory.

case_budget_seconds.description: Remaining control-plane case budget when the client is released.

BenchClientResult.description: The result a Bench client writes for the measurement runtime to consume.

schema_version.description:
Result envelope version; clients write `1`. The measurement runtime
rejects a bench result whose version is not `1`.

BenchDatasetCacheState.description: Read-only cache state observed while resolving the Bench plan.

BenchDatasetCatalogInput.description: Immutable release-catalog facts resolved by the Rust control plane.

BenchDatasetPreparationRequest.description:
The bounded tokenizer-backed operation that materializes one dataset
population before any Bench case starts.

BenchDatasetPreparationResult.description: Terminal result of dataset population materialization.

BenchDefinitionInput.description: The workload shape a Bench client drives, shared across its load cases.

BenchLoadInput.description: How a Bench case paces its requests.

oneOf.description: A fixed number of in-flight requests.
A target arrival rate, optionally shaped by a burstiness factor.
All requests issued as fast as possible.

BenchPopulationInput.description: One frozen dataset population consumed sequentially by every Bench case.

BenchRequestSloInput.description: Per-request latency bounds lowered to the release-owned Bench runner.

BenchRequestSloResult.description: File-bound request-SLO evidence derived from AIPerf profiling records.

BenchRequestSourceInput.description: One closed request origin lowered by Inferlab for the Bench runtime.

oneOf.description:
AIPerf generates exact token-shape prompts from the release-pinned
synthetic generator.

Inferlab materializes a release-catalog conversation snapshot before
AIPerf starts.

BenchTokenCountSummary.description:
Summary of the realized token counts. Exact per-entry values remain in the
population evidence artifact.

BuiltinRouterKind.description: An Inferlab-owned routing implementation with stable target semantics.

CaptureControlRequirement.description: The HTTP paths a capture target exposes to open and close a capture window.

CaptureTargetRequirement.description:
Marks a replica as a profiling capture target and carries its window
control ([[RFC-0004:C-WORKLOAD-PROFILING]]).

ClientEndpointInput.description: The public workload endpoint an Eval or Bench client connects to.

ClientStatus.description: The terminal outcome a measurement client reports.

oneOf.description: The client completed its measurement successfully.
The client did not complete successfully.

EndpointAssignment.description: A concrete host/port endpoint the control plane allocated for a process.

EndpointProtocol.description: The application protocol a workload endpoint speaks.

oneOf.description: HTTP.

EndpointRequirement.description:
The workload endpoint's protocol and named OpenAI paths, plus an optional
prefix-cache-reset action a Bench case can invoke between runs.

EvalClientRequest.description:
The request the Eval measurement runtime passes to its client: the endpoint
to hit, the model, the eval definition, and where to write artifacts.

case_budget_seconds.description: Remaining control-plane case budget when the client is released.

EvalClientResult.description: The result an Eval client writes for the measurement runtime to consume.

schema_version.description:
Result envelope version; clients write `1`. The measurement runtime
rejects an eval result whose version is not `1`.

EvalDefinitionInput.description: The measurement an Eval client runs against the workload endpoint.

oneOf.description: A single-prompt liveness check.
An lm-eval task run with a pass threshold on the chosen metric.

EvalFailureKind.description: A typed Eval failure category preserved across the client boundary.

EvalMetricComparison.description: The threshold comparison selected from lm-eval's scoring direction.

EvalMetricGate.description: The effective threshold comparison for the configured primary metric.

EvalMetricGateConclusion.description: The terminal conclusion of an lm-eval metric threshold gate.

EvalNormalizedMetric.description: One finite lm-eval metric with its exact native provenance.

EvalTaskSourceInput.description: The resolved lm-eval task source consumed by the release-owned Eval runner.

oneOf.description: One individual task shipped by the pinned lm-eval runtime.
One task closure shipped and identity-bound by the Inferlab release.
One validated workspace-local lm-eval YAML file.

EvalTrialSummary.description: Reconstructible aggregate counts for fixed outer Eval trials.

HttpActionSpec.description: An HTTP action (method and path) invoked against the workload endpoint.

oneOf.description: HTTP POST.

IntegrationIdentity.description:
The integration's identity recorded on its results: adapter id, adapter
version, and the framework it lowers to.

KvTransferMechanism.description: The KV-transfer mechanism connecting prefill and decode.

oneOf.description: Mooncake KV-cache transfer.
NIXL KV-cache transfer.

LaunchFileDeclaration.description: One immutable text input a rendered process requires before it can launch.

MeasurementModelInput.description:
The model identity used by measurement clients. Unlike integration
planning, a benchmark client may need a controller-visible tokenizer
locator.

Parallelism.description:
Framework-neutral component-aware parallelism ([[RFC-0003:C-SERVE-PARALLELISM]]).
Every component is optional so an operator can override one without
repeating the rest; omitted components are filled by the integration into a
complete effective shape.

outer.description: Outer deployment parallelism shared by attention and experts.

ParallelismAttention.description: Attention-block parallelism: tensor, data, and context degrees.

ParallelismExperts.description: MoE expert parallelism: tensor, data, expert, and dense-tensor degrees.

ParallelismOuter.description: Outer deployment parallelism: tensor and pipeline degrees.

PlanServeInput.description: The requested serve shape a `PlanServe` operation lowers into a topology.

PlanServeResult.description:
The lowered topology returned by a `PlanServe`: the effective server-level
shape, per-role resolution, whole-replica requirements, role links, and the
public and per-role endpoint requirements the control plane then allocates.

ProcessSpec.description: A launchable process: its argument vector and environment.

ProtocolVersion.description:
The shared protocol version used by framework integrations and release-owned
measurement clients. The only accepted value is `6` (serialized as the
string `"6"`); a mismatch is rejected before lowering.

oneOf.description: Protocol version 6.

RawArtifact.description: A raw output file a client produced, retained as workload evidence.

ReadinessProbe.description: How the control plane decides a process is ready.

oneOf.description: Ready when an HTTP GET of `path` succeeds.

Ready when the public endpoint succeeds and its HTTP target registry
contains every control-plane-derived serving target.
Ready as soon as the process is alive.

RenderInputDeclaration.description:
One workspace-authored UTF-8 source file an integration declares during
planning for the control plane to supply during final rendering.

RenderServeInput.description:
The planned topology plus the control plane's concrete allocations that a
`RenderServe` operation turns into final process invocations.

RenderServeResult.description:
The final process invocations returned by a `RenderServe`, one per supplied
allocation.

RenderedServeProcess.description: A rendered process bound to the allocation `id` it was produced for.

RoutingResult.description: The closed routing owner selected during integration planning.

ServeModelInput.description: The logical model supplied during serving planning and rendering.

ServeProcessAllocation.description:
One concrete process the control plane placed, supplied to `RenderServe`:
its identity, rank, machine, devices, model locator, and allocated
endpoints and named ports.

ServeReplicaRequirement.description:
A whole-replica resource and readiness requirement the integration declares
without choosing placement, ranks, or concrete endpoints.

ServeRoleInput.description:
A requested serving role: its identity, kind, replica cardinality, and
declared (not-yet-completed) parallelism and settings.

ServeRoleKind.description: The logical role a replica plays in a serve topology.

oneOf.description: Aggregated serving role of a single topology.
Prefill role of a disaggregated topology.
Decode role of a disaggregated topology.
Request-routing role that does not execute model inference.

ServeRoleLink.description:
A directed link between serve roles the integration declares as part of the
topology.

oneOf.description: The source role routes requests to the target roles.
KV cache is transferred from source to target over `mechanism`.
The source discovers the target through a bootstrap port.
The source and target exchange out-of-band data over a side-channel port.

ServeRoleResult.description:
A role as the integration resolved it: preserved identity and cardinality
with the complete effective settings and parallelism.

oneOf.description: One aggregated serving role.
Disaggregated prefill and decode roles.

SettingValue.description:
A framework-specific server setting value carried as structured JSON data
(never a pre-rendered shell fragment) across the integration boundary.

anyOf.description: A JSON array of setting values.
A JSON object of named setting values.

SuppliedRenderInput.description:
The original declared path plus the exact UTF-8 contents and digest the
control plane supplies to an integration during final rendering.

TargetEndpointScheme.description: The application protocol used to identify serving targets in a registry.

oneOf.description: HTTP serving endpoint.
gRPC serving endpoint.
